title: cornus disciflora var. floccosa
intro: Cornus disciflora var. floccosa, also known as the drooping dogwood, is a variety of flowering dogwood tree native to China, characterized by its white flowers and woolly branches. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Cornus disciflora var. floccosa include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, tonics, and other medicinal products.
